rulings.Case Title: JULIA MARTINEZ VS. ABLE BUILDING MAINTENANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 16, 2011, line 2, DEFENDANT ABLE BUILDING MAINTENANCE, MARCO HERNANDEZ MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (RETALIATION) IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT DEMONSTRATES A LEGITIMATE BUSINESS REASON FOR THE TRANSFER AND PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT SUBSTANTIAL RESPONSIVE EVIDENCE OF PRETEXT. FURTHER, DEFENDANT HERNANDEZ CANNOT BE LIABLE FOR RETALIATION AS A MATTER OF LAW. JONES V. THE LODGE AT TORREY PINES PARTNERSHIP (2008) 42 CAL.4TH 1158, 1173. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES INTENTIONAL CONDUCT AND PLAINTIFF'S REMEDY IS TO PURSUE THE FEHA CAUSE OF ACTION. OTHERWISE, THE WORKERS' COMPENSATION BAR WOULD APPLY. THE MOTION IS MOOT AS TO THE 5TH, 7TH AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION. IF DEFENDANT SEEKS TO VACATE THE DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE AND HAVE A DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE ENTERED, THEN DEFENDANT SHOULD FILE A FORMAL MOTION. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: KING BUN CHAN VS. FRANK CHOW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 16, 2011, line 6, DEFENDANT FRANK CHOW'S Motion For Attorneys Fees. COURT DENIES CCP 170.6 CHALLENGE. DEFENDANT ALREADY FILED A CHALLENGE TO JUDGE WOOLARD ON NOVEMBER 30, 2010. EACH SIDE ONLY GETS ONE CHALLENGE. SEE CCP 170.6(a)(4). DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES DENIED. THE ACTION, INCLUDING THE NUISANCE NOTICE AND ACTION, WAS BASED IN CONTRACT. UNDER CALIFORNIA CIVIL CODE SECTION 1717(2) IF THE ACTION IS VOLUNTARILY DISMISSED, THERE IS NO PREVAILING PARTY. =(302/LMG)
